title: Dirt Poor Genius
key_lyric: I'm a dirt poor genius
one_line: A male vocalist sings a melancholic folk song about being an unappreciated "dirt poor genius" who struggles financially despite his talents, accompanied by ukulele and guitar.
lyrics:
I'm a dirt poor genius, I teach music through color and way.
I'm a dirt poor genius, and I've got lots of bills to pay.
My family's got a big house, but all they do for me is pray.
My sister is a doctor, her husband and engineer.
My brother is in finance, and I can't even buy me a beer.
I'm a dirt poor genius, I guess I'm just ahead of my time.
I even ask my bed for money, but they couldn't even spare me anyway.
I used to be an engineer, making fuel from coal.
But then I started traveling, it was like music to my soul.
I'm a dirt poor genius, spend every penny that I am.
Well now I teach and lift and pitch, and that's really not all that bad.
I play the ukulele with the colors on the board, to strum or bake a rhythm, every color's a sound is a chord.
I'm a dirt poor genius, is that so hard to see?
I'm a dirt poor genius, I guess at least my mind is free.
I made some silly paintings of the color music code, but I had to put them in storage, no one cared if I burned a load.
I'm a dirt poor genius, why can't I sound a piece of art?
I'm a dirt poor genius, if only I could get paid to be smart.
I had to save some money, just couldn't afford my rent, so I put all my things in storage, but I guess all my luck was spent.
I'm a dirt poor genius, I try not to remember that day.
When I found my treasure just empty, just a little pile of dust for me to play.
My 12 string guitar, Charango, my PC and my back, piano, ukuleles, all the prototypes that I like.
I'm a dirt poor genius, isn't it such a shame?
If found it'll be sold for millions, one day when I am dead rich in fame.
I'm a dirt poor genius, girls don't even look my way.
I'm a dirt poor genius, I guess only one for me is to pay.
One day when I'm rich in fame is they'll pretend to love me night and day.

title: ten minutes he said
key_lyric: She gave him 10 minutes, he took 33, but she gave him a life and he gave her a dream.
one_line: A patient wife supports her coding husband's ambitious work, transforming his promised 'ten minutes' into a lifetime of shared dreams.
lyrics:
10 minutes he said, just 10 minutes please She looked at the clock, she looked at the
keys She said, Lori I swear, if you don't come to bed I will unplug the router and that
will be there But Lori's got a bug, and the bug's got a bug And the guild needs a feature
and the bot needs a hug And the clock says 30, and his wife says his name And the man
who promised 10 minutes cannot feel any shame And Lori is MIA, she's holding the world up
while he's holding control Mia is the reason the man can't even dream She saved his life,
now she saves it every evening by letting him have Just 10 more minutes please
He's juggling the day job, and the guild and the grip And the kids need a lesson, and the
code needs a fix And the meeting ran over, and the founder lies her up And somewhere in the chaos
one woman is enough She doesn't write the code, but she writes the permission She doesn't ship the
features, but she ships the conditions To let a man build something past the hour he should sleep
Mia keeps the ground steady, while Lori goes deep
Cause Mia is patient, and Lori is at my age She counted every minute past the 10 that he promised her today
Mia is the anchor, Lori is the kite She holds the string steady, he flies through the night
And then the string never breaks, cause she tied it just right
He said she saved his life, and he meant every word She's a soulmate, that's not a thing you earn
That's the thing you protect, every single day Even when the code is calling, even when the guild won't wait
So Lori, close the laptop, walk into the room Where the woman who saved you is saving you a spot
On the couch, the code will compile tomorrow She won't wait forever, well actually she will
That's the whole damn point
Mia is patient, and Lori is at my age But he always comes home
At the end of the day, she gave him 10 minutes He took 33, but she gave him a life
And he gave her a dream, and the dream is still building And the building takes time, and the time is a gift
From the woman who's fine, with 10 minutes becoming a lifetime
Mia, he retired 45, he promised, he also promised 10 minutes

title: If (Pink Floyd Cover)
key_lyric: If I were a good man, I'd talk to you more often than I can.
one_line: A melancholic male voice reflects on regrets and possibilities, accompanied by tenderly plucked acoustic guitar.
lyrics:
If I were a swan, I'd be gone
If I were a train, I'd be late
And if I were a good man, I'd talk to you more often than I can
If I were asleep, I could dream
And if I were afraid, I could hide
If I go insane, please don't put your wires in my brain
If I were the moon, I'd be cool
And if I were a role, I would bend for you
If I were a good man, I'd understand the spaces between friends
If I were alone, I would cry
And if I was you, I'd be home and dry
And if I go insane, will you still let me join in with the game?
If I were you, I'd be cool
If I were a swan, I'd be gone
If I were a train, I'd be late again
If I were a good man, I'd talk with you more often than I can

title: If You Go Away
key_lyric: If you go away on this summer day, then you might as well take the sun away.
one_line: A somber folk song features a male vocalist and acoustic guitar, reflecting on the profound sadness of a loved one leaving, comparing their departure to the absence of the sun and the end of cherished memories.
lyrics:
If you look away on this summer day Then you might as well take the sun away
All the birds that flew in the summer sky When our love was near and our hearts were high
When the day was young and the night was long And the moon stood still for the night birds song
If you go away, if you go away, if you go away
But if you stay I'll make you a day Like no day has been or won't be again
We'll sail on the sun, we'll ride on the rain We'll touch the trees, then worship the wind
And if you go, I don't understand Believe me just enough love to fill up my head
And if you go away, if you go away, if you go away, if you go away
As I know you must, there'll be nothing left In the world to trust, just an empty room
Full of empty space, like the empty glue I see on your face, can I tell you now
As you turn to go, I'll be dying slow Till you're next to the love, if you go away, if you go away, if you go away
But if you stay I'll make you a night Like no night has been or won't be again
I'll sail on your smile, I'll ride on your touch I'll touch your eyes, I love you so much
But if you go, now I won't cry as long as it is done From the world goodbye
And if you look away, and if you look away, if you look away, if you go away

Hey, you've got to hide your love away

Gather out all your clowns, let me hear you say
Hey, you've got to hide your love away
Hey, you've got to hide your love away
Hey, you've got to hide your love away
Hey, you've got to hide your love away
